opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My face and scalp are covered in burning, weeping crusts that seem to multiply by the hour. I'm worried it's a terrible infection spreading to my eyes.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It flared badly today and I'm scared it's a flesh-eating or spreading infection.

symptom_narrative.onset: mild for weeks, acutely worse today
symptom_narrative.character: superficial crusted, scaling, weeping patches with mild burning
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: mild burning, clear weeping; no fever; no mouth/eye sores; no lip swelling
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: had scaly patches for weeks (thought to be dandruff/seborrhoeic dermatitis)

objective_data_offered.value: superficial crusted scaly red patches on the face, scalp and upper chest; yellowish crusts (not thick honey-coloured); no intact blisters; mouth and eyes look normal; skin peels superficially if rubbed
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: phone-camera view; cannot perform Nikolsky testing or biopsy remotely
